steps.title: Identify the outcomes the passage just described
steps.content:
The passage lists three patterns: some countries had more women in 2013 than 2009, for some the number in 2013 was less than 2009, and for others it was the same in both years. The blank asks for an example to illustrate these patterns.

Reading the graph: Philippines went from 5 (2009) to 3 (2013); Belgium stayed at 6 both years; Guatemala went from 3 (2009) to 4 (2013).

steps.title: Match the example to the patterns described
steps.content:
Option A directly illustrates the two patterns the passage emphasizes: Philippines decreased (5→3), representing countries where the number was less in 2013, and Belgium stayed the same (6→6), representing countries where it was the same in both years.

Option B shifts to comparing absolute numbers across countries in 2013 rather than changes over time—the passage focuses on how each country changed from 2009 to 2013, not on cross-country comparisons. Options C and D show other combinations (increase + decrease, or increase + same) but don't directly continue the passage's emphasis on demonstrating the decrease and same patterns it just mentioned.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
